items.title: Image and document classification
items.description: k-NN on feature vectors (raw pixels, SIFT, or embedding vectors) assigns labels by similarity to stored examples — useful when labeled data are moderate in size and boundaries are irregular.

items.title: Recommendation and collaborative filtering
items.description: User–item or user–user vectors define neighborhoods; k nearest profiles vote on ratings or item preferences without training a global matrix factorization model.

items.title: Anomaly detection via neighbor distance
items.description: Large average distance to the k nearest training points (or unanimous disagreement with neighbor labels) flags outliers — k-NN structure reused for scoring, not only classification.

items.title: Geospatial and local pricing
items.description: Latitude, longitude, and property attributes define neighbors for estimating local prices, pollution levels, or demand when effects vary smoothly by region.

items.title: Medical diagnosis support from similar cases
items.description: Patient feature vectors matched to k historical cases with known outcomes; clinicians review neighbor profiles — interpretability comes from concrete similar patients, not opaque coefficients.
